messages.text:
<@U06F97BPQJU> Just wanted to make sure you got an update. Summary: Need to try again using more initiator, more activator, and a less deep mold. Also, dropping AESO for the time being. Further details below.

Baker and Andrew made the following samples all at 33% solids loading:
• 10% SBR, 90% AESO
• 10% SBR, 90% AEHOSO
• 20% SBR, 80% AESO
• 20% SBR, 80% AEHOSO
These were poured into a silicone mold with the activator & initiator and placed into a dehydrating oven. When I checked on them this morning, they had developed a clear polymer skin; however, the container was still mostly water. I believe the primary issue was that there was just too much water, and since the mold was a fairly deep mold, the water couldn't dry off appropriately. I'm going to have <@U01LKAFAS58> and <@U012ETLV6NQ> try this again using a cookie sheet or something similar so that the samples are thinner and have higher air flow.

We are also dropping the AESO as an option for the time being. It is worth considering, but from a timing and POC standpoint, it's too much work. We have a large stockpile of AEHOSO, we have to make the AESO. And while it's from commercially sourced ESO, if we don't want to make it every time we'd need to run a larger scale acrylation. We can do that, but it'll take a day or two to be ready, and then a day to run, and for POC that's a time investment wew don't need to make.

<@U012ETLV6NQ> and <@U01LKAFAS58> If you have other thoughts, please let me know. Otherwise, is it possible to get something going on Monday?
